complexity.justification: Table-driven random-access read-then-delete with PERFORM VARYING iteration and error handling on two file operations

annotation_metadata.note: source_lines and cobol_snippet fields checked against the uploaded VSCBEX05.cbl

rules.name: BR_001
rules.confidence_justification: Direct MOVE of a hard-coded literal group into the lookup table structure
rules.natural_language_rule: The program loads a fixed set of 5 customer IDs (0010, 0420, 0878, 0210, 0998), hard-coded at compile time, into the working key table used to drive the deletion run.
rules.precondition: WS-Key-HOLD contains its compile-time VALUE clauses
rules.postcondition: WS-Key-Table (OCCURS 5) is populated with the 5 keys to delete

rules.name: BR_002
rules.confidence_justification: Direct OPEN I-O statement, required because DELETE needs update access
rules.natural_language_rule: The indexed customer file is opened in I-O mode so records can be randomly read and then deleted.
rules.precondition: CUSTFile is defined with ACCESS MODE IS RANDOM and not yet opened
rules.postcondition: CUSTFile is open for both random READ and DELETE

rules.name: BR_003
rules.confidence_justification: PERFORM VARYING...UNTIL is explicit indexed-loop control
rules.cobol_snippet:
PERFORM VARYING WS-Key-SUB FROM 1 BY 1
   UNTIL WS-Key-SUB > WS-Key-Element-Cnt
   MOVE WS-Key-Value(WS-Key-SUB) TO
      CUSTFile-Cust-ID
   PERFORM 5000-Read-CUSTFile
   PERFORM 2100-Delete-CUSTFile
END-PERFORM.
rules.natural_language_rule: For each of the 5 hard-coded customer IDs in order, the program sets the record key, reads that customer's record to position the file, and then deletes it.
rules.precondition: WS-Key-Table-Setup is populated (BR_001) and CUSTFile is open I-O (BR_002)
rules.action: Loop WS-Key-SUB from 1 to WS-Key-Element-Cnt (5); for each, set the key, read the record, then delete it
rules.postcondition: All 5 keyed records have had a read-then-delete cycle attempted

rules.name: BR_004
rules.confidence_justification: DELETE is explicit; error handling is a plain file-status IF/ELSE, not an INVALID KEY clause
rules.cobol_snippet:
6000-Delete-CUSTFile.
    DELETE CUSTFile.
    IF WS-CUSTFile-Good
       ADD +1 TO FD-CUSTFile-Delete-Cnt
    ELSE
       DISPLAY "** ERROR **: 6000-Delete-CUSTFile" ...
       PERFORM 3000-End-Job
       MOVE 8 TO RETURN-CODE
       GOBACK
    END-IF.
rules.natural_language_rule: The record at the current key position is deleted from the indexed customer file. If the delete status is not good, the program logs diagnostics, runs end-of-job cleanup, and terminates immediately with RETURN-CODE 8.
rules.precondition: CUSTFile is positioned at the target record by the preceding READ (BR_003/ER_006)
rules.action: DELETE the current record; on any non-good status, log and abend
rules.postcondition: The record is removed and the counter incremented, or the program has terminated abnormally

rules.name: TR_005
rules.confidence_justification: Direct ADD statement; unambiguous counter increment
rules.natural_language_rule: The system increments the successful-read counter by 1 each time a keyed lookup returns a record prior to deletion.
rules.postcondition: FD-CUSTFile-Record-Cnt reflects the number of successful reads so far

rules.name: TR_006
rules.confidence_justification: Direct DISPLAY statements for the run summary
rules.cobol_snippet:
DISPLAY EOJ-End-Message.
DISPLAY "      Records Read: " FD-CUSTFile-Record-Cnt
DISPLAY "   Records Deleted: " FD-CUSTFile-Delete-Cnt
rules.natural_language_rule: At end of processing, the program displays an end-of-job banner, the total records read, and the total records successfully deleted.
rules.action: DISPLAY end-of-job banner, read count, and delete count
rules.postcondition: Summary information is visible to the operator

rules.name: TR_007
rules.natural_language_rule: The indexed customer file is closed before program termination to release the file handle.

rules.name: ER_008
rules.confidence_justification: READ status check is explicit, but the paragraph combines a not-found/EOF branch with a hard-error abend path
rules.cobol_snippet:
IF WS-CUSTFile-Good
   ADD +1 TO FD-CUSTFile-Record-Cnt
ELSE
   IF WS-CUSTFile-EOF
      NEXT SENTENCE
   ELSE
      DISPLAY "** ERROR **: 5000-Read-CUSTFile" ...
      PERFORM 3000-End-Job
      MOVE 8 TO RETURN-CODE
      GOBACK
   END-IF
END-IF.
rules.natural_language_rule: If the positioning read for a delete target does not succeed, a not-found/EOF condition is treated as a silent skip (NEXT SENTENCE, meaning 2100-Delete-CUSTFile still runs and attempts a DELETE against whatever key is currently positioned), but any other read failure triggers diagnostic display, cleanup, and immediate termination with RETURN-CODE 8.
rules.precondition: A keyed READ against CUSTFile has just been attempted
rules.action: Branch on read status; not-found/EOF falls through to the DELETE attempt anyway, any other error aborts the run
rules.postcondition: Either processing falls through to 2100-Delete-CUSTFile, or the program has terminated abnormally
